clusters.title: Hantavirus-hit cruise ship heads for Canary Islands to evacuate passengers
clusters.neutral_headline: Spain’s Canary Islands brace for incoming hantavirus-stricken cruise ship
bullet_summary: To imply it's a pandemic is dishonest,” they said
Contact tracing is underway in multiple countries including the United States after passengers left the ship before the first reported case

items.title: Hantavirus-hit cruise ship heads for Canary Islands to evacuate passengers
items.standfirst: Spanish authorities on Friday were preparing to receive more than 140 passengers and crew members on board a hantavirus-stricken cruise ship headed for the Canary Islands, where health officials have said they will perform careful evacuations. The vessel is expected to arrive Sunday at the Spanish island of Tenerife, the passengers will then be transported to the airport and flown back to their respective countries.
items.content: Spanish authorities on Friday were preparing to receive more than 140 passengers and crew members on board a hantavirus-stricken cruise ship headed for the Canary Islands, where health officials have said they will perform careful evacuations. The vessel is expected to arrive Sunday at the Spanish island of Tenerife, the passengers will then be transported to the airport and flown back to their respective countries.

items.title: Spain’s Canary Islands brace for incoming hantavirus-stricken cruise ship
items.standfirst: The 140 passengers and crew on the MV Hondius will be 'completely isolated' and evacuated, say Spanish authorities.
items.content: The 140 passengers and crew on the MV Hondius will be 'completely isolated' and evacuated, say Spanish authorities.

items.title: Evacuation of hantavirus-stricken cruise ship could face delays due to bad weather
items.standfirst: ‘Unprecedented operation’ under way to receive MV Hondius off Tenerife to assess and repatriate those onboard• What is hantavirus?• Where did the cruise ship hantavirus come from and what happens next?The evacuation of the hantavirus-stricken MV Hondius cruise ship must be completed within 24 hours of the vessel reaching Tenerife on Sunday or face days or even weeks of delay because of bad weather, authorities in the Canary Islands warned on Friday.The Dutch-flagged vessel, which was sailing from Argentina to Cape Verde, is due to arrive in the Spanish archipelago this weekend, triggering what Spain’s health minister has termed an “unprecedented operation” to receive, assess and repatriate the 149 passengers and crew members onboard. Continue reading...
items.content: ‘Unprecedented operation’ under way to receive MV Hondius off Tenerife to assess and repatriate those onboard• What is hantavirus?• Where did the cruise ship hantavirus come from and what happens next?The evacuation of the hantavirus-stricken MV Hondius cruise ship must be completed within 24 hours of the vessel reaching Tenerife on Sunday or face days or even weeks of delay because of bad weather, authorities in the Canary Islands warned on Friday.The Dutch-flagged vessel, which was sailing from Argentina to Cape Verde, is due to arrive in the Spanish archipelago this weekend, triggering what Spain’s health minister has termed an “unprecedented operation” to receive, assess and repatriate the 149 passengers and crew members onboard. Continue reading...

items.content: The MV Hondius is currently traveling to the Canary Islands, where the 147 people on board will be methodically off-boarded and flown home.

items.content: The U.S. has entered emergency response mode as a cruise ship hit by a deadly hantavirus outbreak sails toward one of Spain’s Canary Islands.

items.title: New plan to evacuate Americans from Hantavirus cruise
items.standfirst: The State Department is arranging a flight from the Canary Islands back to the U.S., where the 17 Americans on board the cruise ship will isolate at the national quarantine unit at Nebraska Medicine in Omaha. NBC News’ Daniele Hamamdjian reports.
items.content: The State Department is arranging a flight from the Canary Islands back to the U.S., where the 17 Americans on board the cruise ship will isolate at the national quarantine unit at Nebraska Medicine in Omaha. NBC News’ Daniele Hamamdjian reports.

items.title: Worldwide race to trace passengers from hantavirus-hit cruise ship

items.content: About 12 countries have so far been linked to the hantavirus outbreak on the MV Hondius, as the UN health agency confirms at least five cases.

items.content: Protesters in Tenerife are voicing opposition to the imminent arrival of a cruise ship hit by hantavirus outbreak

items.title: French couple on hantavirus cruise ship says ‘no panic onboard’
items.standfirst: A French couple who is currently travelling on the MV Hondius, which was recently hit by a hantavirus outbreak, has issued a statement urging media not to “overdramatise” the situation, adding that there is “no panic” among the passengers onboard. "To call this an epidemic is wrong. To imply it's a pandemic is dishonest,” they said.
items.content: A French couple who is currently travelling on the MV Hondius, which was recently hit by a hantavirus outbreak, has issued a statement urging media not to “overdramatise” the situation, adding that there is “no panic” among the passengers onboard. "To call this an epidemic is wrong. To imply it's a pandemic is dishonest,” they said.

items.title: What is hantavirus – and how worried should we be?

items.content: Three passengers have died after being on board a hantavirus-hit cruise ship that had been heading for the Canary Islands.

items.title: Contact tracing underway after passengers left ship before first reported hantavirus case
items.standfirst: The cruise ship at the center of the hantavirus outbreak is heading toward the Canary Islands in Spain. Contact tracing is underway in multiple countries including the United States after passengers left the ship before the first reported case. NBC News' Matt Bodner reports on the latest details from the outbreak.
items.content: The cruise ship at the center of the hantavirus outbreak is heading toward the Canary Islands in Spain. clusters.title: Putin denounces Nato at scaled back Victory Day parade
clusters.neutral_headline: Putin denounces Nato at scaled back Victory Day parade
bullet_summary: Reported by BBC World, Guardian World, Al Jazeera and others

items.title: Putin denounces Nato at scaled back Victory Day parade
items.standfirst: The Russian leader used his annual speech to justify his so-called special military operation in Ukraine.
items.content: The Russian leader used his annual speech to justify his so-called special military operation in Ukraine.

items.title: Russia will always be victorious, says Putin at scaled-back Victory Day parade
items.standfirst: Moscow blanketed in heavy security despite last-minute announcement of three-day ceasefire with UkraineEurope live – latest updatesVladimir Putin has declared Russia will always be victorious as he oversaw a scaled-back Victory Day parade on Red Square held under heavy security amid mounting fears of Ukrainian attacks and growing public fatigue with the war.Speaking to the crowd, the Russian leader invoked the sacrifices of the second world war to rally support for his soldiers fighting in the war in Ukraine. “The great feat of the generation of victors inspires the warriors carrying out the tasks of the special military operation today,” he said, using the Kremlin’s preferred euphemism for his invasion of Ukraine. Continue reading...
items.content: Moscow blanketed in heavy security despite last-minute announcement of three-day ceasefire with UkraineEurope live – latest updatesVladimir Putin has declared Russia will always be victorious as he oversaw a scaled-back Victory Day parade on Red Square held under heavy security amid mounting fears of Ukrainian attacks and growing public fatigue with the war.Speaking to the crowd, the Russian leader invoked the sacrifices of the second world war to rally support for his soldiers fighting in the war in Ukraine. “The great feat of the generation of victors inspires the warriors carrying out the tasks of the special military operation today,” he said, using the Kremlin’s preferred euphemism for his invasion of Ukraine. Continue reading...

items.title: Scaled-back Victory Day parade held in Moscow
items.standfirst: Russia marks downsized Victory Day parade as Putin pledges victory in Ukraine 'special military operation'.
items.content: Russia marks downsized Victory Day parade as Putin pledges victory in Ukraine 'special military operation'.

items.title: Russia marks Victory Day with scaled-back parade
items.standfirst: Russian President Vladimir Putin on Saturday voiced confidence of victory in Ukraine as he oversaw a military parade on Red Square commemorating the defeat of Nazi Germany in World War II. But this year, for the first time in nearly two decades, the parade took place without tanks, missiles and other heavy weapons, aside from a traditional flyover of combat jets over fears of an Ukrainian attack.
items.content: Russian President Vladimir Putin on Saturday voiced confidence of victory in Ukraine as he oversaw a military parade on Red Square commemorating the defeat of Nazi Germany in World War II. But this year, for the first time in nearly two decades, the parade took place without tanks, missiles and other heavy weapons, aside from a traditional flyover of combat jets over fears of an Ukrainian attack.

items.title: Russia holds downsized Victory Day parade
items.standfirst: Putin invoked the Soviet victory in World War II to rally his army in Ukraine, saying 'our heroes move forward'.
items.content: Putin invoked the Soviet victory in World War II to rally his army in Ukraine, saying 'our heroes move forward'.

items.title: Russia is fighting an 'aggressive' NATO-backed force, Putin says in Victory Day speech
items.standfirst: Russian President Vladimir Putin said Saturday that Russian troops in Ukraine were fighting an "aggressive force" backed by all of NATO and described his war goals as "just" in a combative speech during the annual Victory Day parade on Red Square.
items.content: Russian President Vladimir Putin said Saturday that Russian troops in Ukraine were fighting an "aggressive force" backed by all of NATO and described his war goals as "just" in a combative speech during the annual Victory Day parade on Red Square.

items.title: North Korean troops join Putin’s scaled-back parade as Ukraine agrees temporary ceasefire
items.standfirst: There were no neat rows of tanks or ballistic missile carriers rolling down the smooth cobbles of Moscow’s Red Square on Saturday, as Russia scaled back what is usually a grand militaristic Victory Day parade celebrating the Soviet Union’s role in defeating Nazi Germany
items.content: There were no neat rows of tanks or ballistic missile carriers rolling down the smooth cobbles of Moscow’s Red Square on Saturday, as Russia scaled back what is usually a grand militaristic Victory Day parade celebrating the Soviet Union’s role in defeating Nazi Germany

items.title: Rosenberg: Russia's Victory Day parade with no tanks a sign Ukraine war not going to plan
items.standfirst: For the first time in nearly two decades, there will be no military hardware, just soldiers during Saturday's parade in Moscow.
items.content: For the first time in nearly two decades, there will be no military hardware, just soldiers during Saturday's parade in Moscow.

clusters.title: Pentagon releases new files on UFOs
clusters.neutral_headline: Pentagon releases new files on UFOs
bullet_summary: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said in a statement that the documents "have long fueled justified speculation — and it's time the American people see it for themselves
Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said it's time for the American people see it for themselves, as the Pentagon started releasing previously classified documents related to UFOs and UAPs

items.title: Pentagon releases new files on UFOs
items.standfirst: Bright lights and mysterious objects, those are what could be found in a new batch of files on UFOs that the Pentagon began releasing on Friday as President Donald Trump taps into the public's long-held curiosities about "unidentified anomalous phenomena” in the broader universe.
items.content: Bright lights and mysterious objects, those are what could be found in a new batch of files on UFOs that the Pentagon began releasing on Friday as President Donald Trump taps into the public's long-held curiosities about "unidentified anomalous phenomena” in the broader universe.

items.title: Pentagon begins releasing UFO files: "It's time the American people see"
items.standfirst: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said in a statement that the documents "have long fueled justified speculation — and it's time the American people see it for themselves."
items.content: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said in a statement that the documents "have long fueled justified speculation — and it's time the American people see it for themselves."

items.title: 6 videos that stood out in the Pentagon's new UFO drop
items.standfirst: The Pentagon on Friday began releasing hundreds of files on UFOs after President Trump ordered the documents to be made public. CBS News Pentagon reporter Eleanor Watson reviewed the 28 videos in the first batch of files and chose six that stood out to her the most.
items.content: The Pentagon on Friday began releasing hundreds of files on UFOs after President Trump ordered the documents to be made public. CBS News Pentagon reporter Eleanor Watson reviewed the 28 videos in the first batch of files and chose six that stood out to her the most.

items.title: Trump says UFO files released to maximize transparency
items.standfirst: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said it's time for the American people see it for themselves, as the Pentagon started releasing previously classified documents related to UFOs and UAPs. CBS News Pentagon reporter Eleanor Watson has more.
items.content: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said it's time for the American people see it for themselves, as the Pentagon started releasing previously classified documents related to UFOs and UAPs. CBS News Pentagon reporter Eleanor Watson has more.

items.title: Retired Navy fighter pilot who saw UFO urges against racing to conclusions after Pentagon release
items.standfirst: The Department of Defense started releasing previously classified documents related to UFOs on Friday. Lt. Cmdr. Alex Dietrich, a retired Navy fighter pilot who has spoken about her own experience encountering a UFO during a training exercise, joins to discuss.
items.content: The Department of Defense started releasing previously classified documents related to UFOs on Friday. Lt. Cmdr. Alex Dietrich, a retired Navy fighter pilot who has spoken about her own experience encountering a UFO during a training exercise, joins to discuss.

items.title: UFO files spanning decades are released by Defense Department
items.standfirst: Cold War reports of mysterious rotating saucers; recent sightings of metallic elliptical objects floating in mid-air. Those and other reports of unidentified anomalous phenomena or UAPs — the military's term for UFOs — are described in documents released Friday.
items.content: Cold War reports of mysterious rotating saucers; recent sightings of metallic elliptical objects floating in mid-air. Those and other reports of unidentified anomalous phenomena or UAPs — the military's term for UFOs — are described in documents released Friday.(Image credit: NASA)

items.content: The Defense Department has released a new trove of declassified documents about government UFO sightings.

items.title: Trump says Russia and Ukraine have agreed to his request for a 3-day ceasefire

items.content: President Volodymyr Zelenskyy and Yuri Ushakov, President Vladimir Putin's foreign affairs adviser, both confirmed the agreement for a three-day ceasefire and an exchange of prisoners.(Image credit: Jacquelyn Martin)

items.title: Three-day ceasefire between Russia and Ukraine announced
items.standfirst: A three-day ceasefire has been agreed by the leaders of Russia and Ukraine, as well as an exchange of prisoners, following a request from Donald Trump.
items.content: A three-day ceasefire has been agreed by the leaders of Russia and Ukraine, as well as an exchange of prisoners, following a request from Donald Trump.

items.title: Trump announces three-day ceasefire between Ukraine and Russia
items.standfirst: US President Donald Trump on Friday announced a three-day ceasefire between Russia and Ukraine, starting on May 9. Aside from a pause in the fighting, the two warring sides will also engage in a prisoner swap, with 1,000 prisoners from each country.
items.content: US President Donald Trump on Friday announced a three-day ceasefire between Russia and Ukraine, starting on May 9. Aside from a pause in the fighting, the two warring sides will also engage in a prisoner swap, with 1,000 prisoners from each country.

items.title: Three hikers dead after Indonesia’s Mount Dukono volcano erupts
items.standfirst: Two Singaporeans and a local person were in no-go zone when they were killed, say officialsThree hikers – two Singaporeans and an Indonesian – have died in an eruption of Indonesia’s Mount Dukono volcano, where they found themselves in a no-go zone, officials said.The eruption, on Halmahera island, sent an ash cloud about 6 miles (10km) into the air, with no towns or villages near enough to face any immediate threat. clusters.title: South Africa’s highest court revives impeachment proceedings against the president Cyril Ramaphosa
clusters.neutral_headline: South Africa president faces call to resign after court ruling
bullet_summary: South Africa's top court has found against president Cyril Ramaphosa over the "Farmgate" scandal
Reported by France24, BBC World, Sky News World

items.title: South Africa’s highest court revives impeachment proceedings against the president Cyril Ramaphosa
items.standfirst: South Africa's President is facing the possibilty of impeachment, in a long running scandal known as Farmgate. It involves the dissapearance of over half a million dollars from Cyril Ramaposa's luxury farmhouse, which was stolen in a breakin in 2020. Ramaphosa is accused of serious violations and misconduct, and the Constitutional court has lifted an earlier ruling that blocked proceedings. He denies any wrongdoing.
items.content: South Africa's President is facing the possibilty of impeachment, in a long running scandal known as Farmgate. It involves the dissapearance of over half a million dollars from Cyril Ramaposa's luxury farmhouse, which was stolen in a breakin in 2020. Ramaphosa is accused of serious violations and misconduct, and the Constitutional court has lifted an earlier ruling that blocked proceedings. He denies any wrongdoing.

items.title: South Africa president faces call to resign after court ruling
items.standfirst: The Constitutional Court says MPs were was wrong to block impeachment proceedings against Cyril Ramaphosa.
items.content: The Constitutional Court says MPs were was wrong to block impeachment proceedings against Cyril Ramaphosa.

items.title: 'Farmgate' scandal could bring down South African president
items.standfirst: South Africa's top court has found against president Cyril Ramaphosa over the "Farmgate" scandal.

clusters.title: Could Labour and Conservative party dominance in UK politics be ending?
clusters.neutral_headline: Could Labour and Conservative party dominance in UK politics be ending
bullet_summary: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer said Friday he will not resign after local elections that saw his governing Labour Party suffer big losses and the hard-right Reform UK make important gains
Reported by Al Jazeera, France24, NBC World

items.title: Could Labour and Conservative party dominance in UK politics be ending?
items.standfirst: Britain's prime minister is under pressure to quit after huge losses in local elections.
items.content: Britain's prime minister is under pressure to quit after huge losses in local elections.

items.title: British PM Starmer says he won't quit after local elections drubbing
items.standfirst: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer said Friday he will not resign after local elections that saw his governing Labour Party suffer big losses and the hard-right Reform UK make important gains. The local elections were widely seen as an unofficial referendum on Starmer.
items.content: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer said Friday he will not resign after local elections that saw his governing Labour Party suffer big losses and the hard-right Reform UK make important gains. The local elections were widely seen as an unofficial referendum on Starmer.

items.title: Substantial win for Nigel Farage's Reform UK in local elections
items.standfirst: The labor party is on the ropes in England as the results of local elections being counted show the far-right making substantial gains. The governing party has lost control of a number of councils... and pressure is piling on Keir Starmer -- with some calling for the Prime Minister to resign. Details by FRANCE 24's correspondent in London, Bénédicte Paviot.
items.content: The labor party is on the ropes in England as the results of local elections being counted show the far-right making substantial gains. The governing party has lost control of a number of councils... and pressure is piling on Keir Starmer -- with some calling for the Prime Minister to resign. Details by FRANCE 24's correspondent in London, Bénédicte Paviot.

items.title: Hard-right party routs Starmer’s Labour in U.K. elections as Trump ally hails ‘historic shift’
items.standfirst: Early results Friday from nationwide elections in Britain suggested a historic drubbing for Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s Labour Party and sweeping gains for hard-right Reform U.K., led by Trump ally Nigel Farage.
items.content: Early results Friday from nationwide elections in Britain suggested a historic drubbing for Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s Labour Party and sweeping gains for hard-right Reform U.K., led by Trump ally Nigel Farage.

clusters.title: 'Clean break' with Orban: Hungary's Magyar to usher in 'new era'
clusters.neutral_headline: Magyar to succeed Orban as Hungary's new prime minister
bullet_summary: "It's officially a new era", said Zsolt Kerner, senior reporter at Hungarian online media 24
Reported by France24, BBC World

items.title: 'Clean break' with Orban: Hungary's Magyar to usher in 'new era'
items.standfirst: Hungary's incoming prime minister Pater Magyar is marking a "clean break" with his predecessor Viktor Orban as he is set to be sworn into office on Saturday. "It's officially a new era", said Zsolt Kerner, senior reporter at Hungarian online media 24.hu. Magyar was previously a member of Orban's Fidesz party.
items.content: Hungary's incoming prime minister Pater Magyar is marking a "clean break" with his predecessor Viktor Orban as he is set to be sworn into office on Saturday. "It's officially a new era", said Zsolt Kerner, senior reporter at Hungarian online media 24.hu. Magyar was previously a member of Orban's Fidesz party.

items.title: Magyar to succeed Orban as Hungary's new prime minister
items.standfirst: Pro-European conservative Peter Magyar arrived at Hungary's parliament Saturday morning to be sworn in as prime minister, on a promise of "regime change" after nationalist Viktor Orban's 16 years in power. The former government insider-turned-critic scored a landslide victory last month, pledging wide-ranging reforms to fight corruption.
items.content: Pro-European conservative Peter Magyar arrived at Hungary's parliament Saturday morning to be sworn in as prime minister, on a promise of "regime change" after nationalist Viktor Orban's 16 years in power. The former government insider-turned-critic scored a landslide victory last month, pledging wide-ranging reforms to fight corruption.

items.title: Hungary’s Magyar set to be sworn in as new prime minister
items.standfirst: Hungary's incoming prime minister, Peter Magyar, arrived Saturday at the Parliament building to be sworn into office, ending Viktor Orban's autocratic 16-year rule. RFI Correspondent Florence Le Bruyère reports from Budapest.
items.content: Hungary's incoming prime minister, Peter Magyar, arrived Saturday at the Parliament building to be sworn into office, ending Viktor Orban's autocratic 16-year rule. RFI Correspondent Florence Le Bruyère reports from Budapest.

items.title: Hungary's new PM to be sworn in during 'regime change' party
items.standfirst: Nearly a month ago Péter Magyar steered his Tisza party to a landslide victory, sweeping away 16 years of rule by Viktor Orbán.
items.content: Nearly a month ago Péter Magyar steered his Tisza party to a landslide victory, sweeping away 16 years of rule by Viktor Orbán.

items.title: Magyar sworn in as prime minister as Hungary turns the page on Orban
items.standfirst: Hungary’s parliament was set to swear in opposition leader Peter Magyar as prime minister on Saturday, marking the end of Viktor Orban’s 16 years in power after the pro-European conservative challenger won a landslide election victory on promises to tackle corruption and restore democratic institutions.
items.content: Hungary’s parliament was set to swear in opposition leader Peter Magyar as prime minister on Saturday, marking the end of Viktor Orban’s 16 years in power after the pro-European conservative challenger won a landslide election victory on promises to tackle corruption and restore democratic institutions.

clusters.title: UK local elections: "Starmer must address voters' frustration over change"
clusters.neutral_headline: British leader Keir Starmer under pressure after heavy election losses
bullet_summary: Number 10 said:The PM has committed to boosting the country’s security and resilience’In this role, Gordon Brown will advise on how global finance cooperation can help to achieve this
Reported by France24, Guardian World

items.title: UK local elections: "Starmer must address voters' frustration over change"
items.standfirst: Britain’s Prime Minister Keir Starmer is under growing pressure after Labour suffered major setbacks in local elections across England, while also struggling in Wales and Scotland as Reform UK continued to gain support. The results have raised fresh questions about Starmer’s leadership less than a year after leading Labour back into government. France 24’s Gavin Lee speaks to James Lyons, who served under Starmer in Downing Street and previously worked as political editor at the Daily Mirror and The Sunday Times.
items.content: Britain’s Prime Minister Keir Starmer is under growing pressure after Labour suffered major setbacks in local elections across England, while also struggling in Wales and Scotland as Reform UK continued to gain support. The results have raised fresh questions about Starmer’s leadership less than a year after leading Labour back into government. France 24’s Gavin Lee speaks to James Lyons, who served under Starmer in Downing Street and previously worked as political editor at the Daily Mirror and The Sunday Times.

items.title: Starmer brings in Gordon Brown and Harriet Harman to ease pressure on him to resign
items.standfirst: Brown to advise on global finance, while Harman will focus on social and economic improvements for women and girlsUK politics live – latest updatesFull results from England, Scotland and WalesKeir Starmer has brought in Gordon Brown and Harriet Harman as advisersin a move to ease the mounting pressure on the prime minister to resign after the disastrous election results for Labour.Brown, the former prime minister and long-serving chancellor under Tony Blair, has been made Starmer’s envoy on global finance, with a brief to advise on financial partnerships to help with defence-related investments, particularly with Europe. Continue reading...

items.title: British leader Keir Starmer under pressure after heavy election losses
items.standfirst: Labour party loses more than 1,400 English council seats and crashes out in Welsh and Scottish parliament votes• UK politics live – latest updatesKeir Starmer, the British prime minister, is facing increasing pressure to set a date for his departure after elections across much of the country resulted in massive losses for his ruling Labour party.With the bulk of results now counted after voting on Thursday, Labour had lost more than 1,400 representatives from English councils, the local government structures that deliver many neighbourhood services. Continue reading...

Continue reading...

items.title: Labour suffers losses across the UK as Reform surges: “Starmer’s survival depends on improvement”
items.standfirst: Britain’s Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer is under growing pressure after Labour’s bruising local election results. The party lost hundreds of council seats in England, fell behind Plaid Cymru in Wales as Reform UK surged, and continued to struggle in Scotland where the Scottish National Party remains dominant. Reform UK has made major gains across England, Wales and Scotland, raising fresh questions over Starmer’s leadership and Labour’s direction. France 24’s Gavin Lee is joined by Tom Baldwin, former Labour Head of Communications and author of Keir Starmer: The Biography, to discuss the fallout from Labour’s losses and whether pressure is already building within the party to replace the Prime Minister.
items.content: Britain’s Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer is under growing pressure after Labour’s bruising local election results. The party lost hundreds of council seats in England, fell behind Plaid Cymru in Wales as Reform UK surged, and continued to struggle in Scotland where the Scottish National Party remains dominant. Reform UK has made major gains across England, Wales and Scotland, raising fresh questions over Starmer’s leadership and Labour’s direction. France 24’s Gavin Lee is joined by Tom Baldwin, former Labour Head of Communications and author of Keir Starmer: The Biography, to discuss the fallout from Labour’s losses and whether pressure is already building within the party to replace the Prime Minister.
